server: Render free tier (512 MB RAM, 0.1 CPU), region Singapore
client: residential connection, India (Windows 11, Python 3.10 requests, this repo's scripts)

cold_start.spin_down_verification: Service idle to external traffic for 20.8 min after going live (only Render's internal /health checker every 5s). First request took 32.5 s vs 0.13-0.23 s immediately after: unmistakably cold. Conclusion: Render's internal health checks do NOT keep a free instance warm, so cold start is measurable with the /health check path enabled.

warm.payloads: varied realistic profiles, seeded rng(42), categories drawn from src.config.CATEGORICAL_DOMAINS

notes: Latency includes India->Singapore RTT plus model inference on 0.1 CPU. Measured BEFORE any Prometheus scraping exists (NOTES.md phase-ordering constraint): once Phase 5 points Prometheus at the live /metrics endpoint the service stays permanently warm and cold starts become unobservable.
